comments.author: @Jimages_uk
comments.text:
In the past there were a number of cinema's in Belize City, I think at least 4 or 5.  The Palace theatre,  Majestic, Eden, and Bel Rio, I'm sure there was at least one more, but the name escapes me at the moment.  But the introduction of cable TV meant they eventually became unused.

The swing birdge has not been turned for ages now, and is being replaced in the near future. It is a landmark of Belize City, and for that reason, it will be missed, but it is no longer fit for purpose, so the new bridge will benefit those who cross the river.  It will not be a swing bridge again.

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Thank you so much for sharing these wonderful memories and historical details! It’s fascinating to hear about the golden era of cinemas like The Palace and Majestic before cable TV changed everything. Also, it’s sad but understandable to hear about the fate of the iconic Swing Bridge. Change is inevitable for progress, but those landmarks will always hold a special place in the city's history. Truly appreciate your insight!

comments.author: @mackenziewhyte610
comments.text: wHAT ABOUT THE MAGAROOGU WATER ?

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: That’s an excellent question! There are so many fascinating hidden details and local stories surrounding the water sources there that we couldn't fit into this single documentary. We’d love to explore and share more about that in our future projects. Thanks for bringing it up!

comments.author: @msanashka
comments.text: PATTAYA ?   That's not Belize!!  Please don't include photos from other countries.  Misleading and misinformation.  I'm originally from there!  There is no McDonald's  Fried Chicken , sure, local is best!!

replies.author: @Jimages_uk
replies.text: I remember when there was a KFC in Belize City, it didn't last very long though.

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Thank you so much for pointing this out, and please accept my sincere apologies! There was an oversight during the final editing stage where that stock footage of Pattaya accidentally slipped in. I truly appreciate you keeping me accountable—I'll be much more thorough moving forward to deliver the highest quality content for our community. Thanks for sharing love for your home country!

comments.author: @dioncrown5966
comments.text: About 50 percent, give or take, is a bunch of nonsense. Also... It's funny how AI pronounces some of the words.

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Appreciate the honest feedback! Balancing different sources for these hidden facts can definitely be tricky, but we're always trying to refine the info. As for the AI voice—yeah, it definitely has some hilarious moments with local names and unique words! 🤖 Thanks for watching and keeping us on our toes!

comments.author: @richardburns6305
comments.text: Belize has traffic lights but not many like most modern countries A good old speed bump cures speeding.

replies.author: @timcummings6147
replies.text: The capital city of Belmopan only has one traffic light and the ring road around the city is 2.5 miles long. It's funny no one believes you about the speed bumps until they actually encounter them. Belize speed bumps kick sand in the face everyone else's speed bumps.

comments.author: @richardburns6305
comments.text: They are getting rid if that old Swing Bridge. A Japanese Construction Firm has already been contracted to build the new bridge. The problem is that Belize City is split into two by a river and they canmot just remove the old bridge without a secondary / temporary bridge in place at that location.

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Thanks for dropping all these fantastic insider updates! 🇧🇿 Things on the ground can evolve so fast, so if any details in the video missed the absolute latest shifts, I appreciate your understanding. Your insights add so much value to the community—thanks for keeping us informed!
